subject: Anthropology
title: Depression Across Cultures
description: A fast breakdown of Depression Across Cultures for builders.

hook.text: In China, depression often appears as back pain, not sadness. The body speaks when the culture won't name the feeling.

buildup.text: Depression is a global phenomenon, but its symptoms, causes, and meanings differ profoundly across cultural contexts.

discovery.text: In many cultures, depression shows as bodily complaints — headaches, fatigue, and pain rather than emotional words.

twist.text: Western psychiatry centers sadness. But in many places, social withdrawal, anger, or physical symptoms are key signs.

climax.text: Depression is universal in some form, but its face changes with culture. Effective treatment must speak the local idiom.

quiz.question: How does depression often appear in non-Western cultures?
options: Often as bodily complaints like pain and fatigue
It doesn't exist outside the West
